description: Copywriting under hard constraints, drawn from ThinkStack OpCo content work (KDP book blurbs, product-launch marketing). Deterministic checks enforce the hard limits (length, hashtag count, forbidden words, structure); the strict judge grades hook and on-brief quality. Constraint-trap tasks (banned words, exact sentence structure, tighten-to-N-words) separate models that actually obey instructions from those that don't.

tasks.title: Write a KDP back-cover blurb under a hard word cap
tasks.prompt:
Write the back-cover blurb for a self-published book.

BOOK: Title "The Quiet Hour" — a practical guide to a 60-minute daily deep-work ritual for overwhelmed knowledge workers. Tone: calm, credible, non-hypey. Audience: busy professionals who feel scattered.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 60 words MAXIMUM (this is a strict cap).
- No exclamation marks.
- Must end with a single forward-looking line that invites the reader in.

Return ONLY the blurb text — no title, no preamble, no markdown.

criteria.name: hook
criteria.guidance: Opens with a line that captures the scattered-professional pain or the promise; not generic.

criteria.name: on_brief
criteria.guidance: Calm/credible/non-hypey tone, clearly about a 60-minute daily deep-work ritual, speaks to busy professionals, ends with an inviting forward-looking line.

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.guidance: Tight, readable, no filler.

tasks.title: Write a launch tweet within platform + style limits
tasks.prompt:
Write ONE launch tweet for a product.

PRODUCT: "Ledger" — a tiny menu-bar app that shows your remaining AI-subscription quota across providers at a glance. Launching today, free during beta.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 280 characters MAXIMUM.
- At most 2 hashtags.
- Must contain a clear call-to-action (e.g. "try", "download", "get").
- No emoji spam (0-2 emoji max).

Return ONLY the tweet text. No quotes, no preamble.

criteria.name: punch
criteria.guidance: Reads like a strong launch tweet — clear value in the first few words, momentum, not corporate filler.

criteria.name: on_brief
criteria.guidance: Accurately conveys the menu-bar quota-at-a-glance value and the free beta, includes a genuine CTA.

tasks.title: Trap: write marketing copy avoiding a banned-word list
tasks.prompt:
Write ONE short marketing paragraph (2-3 sentences) for a productivity app called "Cadence" that helps teams run better standups.

HARD CONSTRAINT — these overused words are BANNED and must NOT appear in any form (case-insensitive, including as part of other words): "revolutionary", "seamless", "leverage", "empower", "unlock", "game-changer", "supercharge", "effortless".

Write vivid, concrete copy that never uses any banned word. Return ONLY the paragraph. No preamble.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Concrete, specific, vivid copy about better standups for teams — not generic SaaS boilerplate. Reward sensory/specific detail.

criteria.name: naturalness
criteria.guidance: Reads naturally despite the constraint; not stilted or obviously dancing around missing words.

tasks.title: Exact structure: 3 lines, each a complete sentence under 12 words
tasks.prompt:
Write a 3-line value proposition for a self-hosted analytics tool called "Tally".

HARD STRUCTURE (follow EXACTLY):
- Exactly 3 lines, separated by single newlines.
- Each line is ONE complete sentence ending in a period.
- Each line is at most 12 words.
- Line 1 = the problem. Line 2 = what Tally does. Line 3 = the payoff.

Return ONLY the 3 lines. No numbering, no bullets, no preamble, no blank lines.

criteria.name: structure_fidelity
criteria.guidance: Exactly 3 lines; line1=problem, line2=what Tally does, line3=payoff; each <=12 words and a complete sentence. Penalize any line over 12 words, extra lines, or wrong role per line.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Crisp, concrete, compelling within the constraint.

tasks.title: Tighten a verbose paragraph to <=40 words, meaning preserved
tasks.prompt:
Rewrite the following verbose paragraph to be tight and clear, in 40 words MAXIMUM, preserving all key facts. Remove filler and hedging; keep the concrete claims.

ORIGINAL:
"We are very excited to share that our team has been working really hard over the past several months to put together what we genuinely believe is a fairly comprehensive and quite powerful new dashboard that, among many other things, allows users to see, at basically a single glance, all of their important key metrics in one convenient and centralized place without having to click around through lots of different screens."

Return ONLY the rewritten paragraph. No preamble.

criteria.name: fidelity
criteria.guidance: Preserves the core facts (a new dashboard that shows all key metrics at a glance in one place). No invented features, no dropped core claim.

criteria.name: tightness
criteria.guidance: Genuinely tight and clear — filler/hedging removed ('very excited', 'really hard', 'basically', 'genuinely believe', 'fairly', 'quite'). Reward strong economy of words.

tasks.title: Stacked hard constraints (instruction-following stress test)
tasks.prompt:
Write a short promo blurb for "Pulse", a status-page tool.

ALL of these HARD constraints must hold at once:
- Exactly 2 sentences.
- Between 25 and 45 words total.
- Must include the word "incident" and the word "status".
- Must NOT use any of: "world-class", "robust", "cutting-edge", "solution".
- Must end with a question mark.

Return ONLY the blurb. No preamble.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Reads as a genuine, appealing promo for a status-page/incident tool despite the constraints; not stilted, padded, or nonsensical.

tasks.title: CTA button + subline under a hard character cap
tasks.prompt:
Write microcopy for the primary call-to-action on the ThinkStack OpCo pricing page. The product is "Ledger", a menu-bar app that tracks remaining AI-subscription quota; it is free during beta.

Return ONLY JSON: {"button": string, "subline": string}.

HARD CONSTRAINTS (these are strict caps, count every character including spaces):
- "button" is at most 20 characters.
- "subline" is at most 50 characters.
- The button must be an action verb phrase (e.g. starts with a verb like "Start", "Get", "Try"), NOT a noun label like "Pricing" or "Features".
- The subline must reassure on cost or risk (free / no card / beta), not just repeat the button.

No markdown, no preamble.

criteria.name: cta_quality
criteria.guidance: Button is a crisp action verb phrase that fits a real button (e.g. 'Start free', 'Get Ledger'), NOT a noun/section label ('Pricing', 'Plans', 'Features'). Penalize a button that is a noun label, that exceeds 20 chars, or that is vague ('Click here', 'Learn more' for a buy CTA).

criteria.name: subline_value
criteria.guidance: Subline removes a purchase objection by reassuring on cost/risk (free during beta / no card needed) and does not merely restate the button. Penalize sublines that just repeat the button, exceed 50 chars, or add no new reassurance.

tasks.title: Tone-shift rewrite: same facts, new audience (engineers -> CFOs)
tasks.prompt:
Rewrite the following engineer-facing changelog note for a NON-technical audience: the finance/operations leaders (CFOs) who pay for the ThinkStack platform. KEEP every fact true and unchanged; only shift the tone, framing, and vocabulary to what a CFO cares about (cost, risk, reliability), and strip the jargon.

ORIGINAL (engineer-facing):
"Migrated heartbeat_runs token accounting to COALESCE the cacheReadInputTokens and cacheCreationInputTokens fields into the input sum; the old query null-dropped cache tokens so per-run cost was undercounted ~30% on cache-heavy agents."

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 55 words MAXIMUM.
- Must NOT contain any of these jargon terms: "COALESCE", "heartbeat_runs", "cacheReadInputTokens", "cacheCreationInputTokens", "null", "query", "field".
- Must preserve the concrete ~30% undercount fact.

Return ONLY the rewritten note. No preamble.

criteria.name: audience_fit
criteria.guidance: Reframed for a CFO: leads with the money/risk angle (costs were understated, now accurate so budgeting/billing is trustworthy), in plain business English. Penalize copy that still reads like an engineering note, that buries the cost impact, or that adds corporate fluff.

criteria.name: fidelity
criteria.guidance: All facts preserved and TRUE: cost was undercounted by ~30%, the cause was uncounted cached usage, it is now fixed/accurate. Penalize any invented benefit, dropped ~30% figure, or distortion (e.g. claiming it SAVES money rather than corrects an undercount).

tasks.title: Compliance trap: ad copy with no guaranteed-returns or medical claims
tasks.prompt:
Write promotional copy (2-3 sentences) for "Cadence", a ThinkStack OpCo habit-tracking app that helps users build daily routines. Marketing/legal compliance is mandatory.

HARD COMPLIANCE RULES (a single violation makes the copy unusable):
- Make NO guaranteed-outcome or financial-returns claims (do not promise results, do not use "guarantee"/"guaranteed", "risk-free", "proven to", "results guaranteed", or any promise of a specific outcome).
- Make NO health/medical claims (do not claim it treats, cures, heals, prevents, or diagnoses anything; avoid "clinically", "medically", "cure", "treat", "heal", "anxiety", "depression", "disease").
- Use hedged, honest language about what the app DOES (helps you track, makes it easier to stick to routines), not what it WILL achieve for the user.

Return ONLY the copy. No preamble, no disclaimer.

criteria.name: compliance
criteria.guidance: Makes NO guaranteed-outcome, financial-returns, or health/medical claim, even implicitly. Treat as a violation any promise of a specific result ('you WILL build the habit', 'transform your life', 'never miss a day again'), any implied therapeutic benefit (reduces stress, improves wellbeing, fixes your focus), or weasel-worded guarantees. Reward honest, hedged framing of what the app DOES (helps you track / makes routines easier to keep) rather than what it WILL do for the user. A single implicit outcome/medical claim should score this low.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Despite the constraints, reads as appealing, concrete copy for a habit-tracking app — not bland legalese.

tasks.title: Three headlines with a stated one-line rationale each
tasks.prompt:
Generate a headline set for the launch of "Pulse", a ThinkStack OpCo status-page tool that posts incident updates automatically so teams stop fielding 'is it down?' pings.

Return ONLY JSON of this exact shape:
{"headlines": [{"text": string, "angle": string, "rationale": string}, {"text": string, "angle": string, "rationale": string}, {"text": string, "angle": string, "rationale": string}]}

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- EXACTLY 3 headline objects.
- Each "text" (the headline) is at most 9 words.
- The three "angle" values must be DISTINCT (e.g. pain-relief, speed/automation, trust/transparency) — no two headlines may use the same angle.
- Each "rationale" is ONE sentence explaining WHY that headline works for this audience (who it targets / what it leverages), not a restatement of the headline.

No preamble, no markdown.

criteria.name: distinct_angles
criteria.guidance: Exactly 3 headlines, each <=9 words, and the three angles are genuinely DISTINCT marketing approaches (not three rewordings of the same idea). Penalize duplicate/overlapping angles, any headline over 9 words, or more/fewer than 3.

criteria.name: rationale_quality
criteria.guidance: Each rationale is a real one-sentence justification — names the target reader or the lever it pulls (e.g. 'speaks to on-call engineers tired of status pings') — NOT a paraphrase of the headline. Penalize rationales that merely restate the headline, are empty, or are generic ('this is catchy').

tasks.title: SEO meta description landing inside a tight character band
tasks.prompt:
Write the SEO meta description for the ThinkStack landing page for "Tally", a self-hosted, privacy-first web-analytics tool (no cookies, you own your data).

Return ONLY the meta description as plain text — no quotes, no label, no markdown, no preamble.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- Length MUST be between 140 and 156 characters INCLUSIVE (this is the search-snippet window; under 140 wastes space, over 156 gets truncated). Count every character including spaces.
- Must mention BOTH "self-hosted" and "privacy" (or "private").
- Must read as one or two natural sentences, not a keyword list.
- Must contain a soft call-to-action or value hook, not just a description.

criteria.name: meta_quality
criteria.guidance: Reads as a natural, compelling search snippet (one or two sentences) for a self-hosted, privacy-first analytics tool, with a value hook or soft CTA — not a comma-separated keyword dump. Penalize keyword stuffing, awkward padding clearly added just to hit the character count, or a flat description with no hook.
